Perfect! I now have comprehensive data. Let me compile the research findings.

```yaml
topic: Agentic AI Market Size, Growth Projections, and Segmentation (2025-2032)
summary: The agentic AI market is experiencing explosive growth with 2025 base-year values ranging from $6.96B–$7.63B across major research firms, projected to reach $57B–$182B by 2031–2033 with CAGRs between 42%–49.6%. Enterprise automation, LLM reasoning breakthroughs, and cloud infrastructure maturation are primary drivers. Deployment is dominated by cloud (59.7%), solutions (61.6%), and BFSI/retail verticals, with significant demand for customer service, data analytics, and autonomous process automation functions.

findings:
  - heading: Market Size Estimates from Major Research Firms (2025 Base Year)
    details: |
      Multiple research firms have published agentic AI market estimates for 2025 with notable variance:
      
      • Grand View Research: $7.63B (2025) → $10.91B (2026) → $182.97B (2033), CAGR 49.6% (2026-2033)
      • MarketsandMarkets: $7.06B (2025) → $93.20B (2032), CAGR 44.6% (2025-2032) | Note: Base year 2024 at $4.81B
      • Mordor Intelligence: $6.96B (2025) → $9.89B (2026) → $57.42B (2031), CAGR 42.14% (2026-2031)
      • Statista: Market projected to surpass $47B by 2030, CAGR >44%
      • Fortune Business Insights: $7.29B (2025) → $9.14B (2026) → $139.19B (2034), CAGR 40.5%
      • IndustryARC: $121B by 2032, CAGR 42% (2026-2032)
      • Coherent Market Insights: $9.87B (2026) → $114.89B (2033)
      • Information Matters: $7.3B (2025) → $52B (2030)
      
      Range: 2025 base year $6.96B–$7.63B; 2030 forecast $42.56B–$52B; 2032-2033 forecast $93.2B–$182.97B
      Most consensus CAGR: 40.5%–49.6% across forecast horizons
    sources:
      - title: AI Agents Market Size And Share | Industry Report, 2033
        url: https://www.grandviewresearch.com/industry-analysis/ai-agents-market-report
      - title: Agentic AI Market Report 2025-2032, By Offering, Geo, Tech
        url: https://www.marketsandmarkets.com/Market-Reports/agentic-ai-market-208190735.html
      - title: Agentic AI Market Share, Size & Growth Outlook to 2031
        url: https://www.mordorintelligence.com/industry-reports/agentic-ai-market
      - title: Global market value of agentic AI 2030 - Statista
        url: https://www.statista.com/statistics/1552183/global-agentic-ai-market-value/
      - title: Agentic AI Market Size, Share | Forecast Report [2026-2034]
        url: https://www.fortunebusinessinsights.com/agentic-ai-market-114233
      - title: Agentic AI Market Size, Share | Industry Trend & Forecast 2030
        url: https://www.industryarc.com/PressRelease/5365/agentic-ai-market

  - heading: Market Segmentation by Vertical/Industry
    details: |
      Vertical market adoption shows significant concentration in enterprise sectors with highest early adoption:
      
      BFSI (Banking, Financial Services, Insurance): 19.12% market share (2025, Mordor), leading driver of adoption
        - Use cases: fraud detection, credit scoring, automated claims processing, compliance automation, portfolio risk simulation
      
      Retail & E-Commerce: Major vertical with dynamic pricing optimization, intelligent inventory management, personalized merchandising
      
      Manufacturing: 45.10% CAGR through 2031 (Mordor), second-fastest growing; smart factory operations, predictive maintenance, supply chain optimization
      
      Healthcare: $538.51M (2024) → $4.96B (2030) per Grand View Research, CAGR 35% est.
        - Applications: clinical trial workflow optimization, diagnostic imaging, personalized treatment planning, hospital resource orchestration
      
      Professional Services: Highest growth vertical for proposal/RFP generation with 57.2% CAGR (MarketsandMarkets forecast period 2025-2032)
        - Use cases: contract review, legal drafting, audit workflow management, generative design
      
      Telecommunications, Software & Technology Providers, Government & Defense, Automotive, Energy & Utilities also showing significant traction
      
      Data point: North America leads with 39.6%-40.25% share (2025); Asia-Pacific is fastest-growing region at 44.95% CAGR (Mordor)
    sources:
      - title: Agentic AI Market Share, Size & Growth Outlook to 2031
        url: https://www.mordorintelligence.com/industry-reports/agentic-ai-market
      - title: Agentic AI Market Report 2025- 2032, By Offering, Geo, Tech
        url: https://www.marketsandmarkets.com/Market-Reports/agentic-ai-market-208190735.html
      - title: Agentic AI In Healthcare Market Size | Industry Report, 2030
        url: https://www.grandviewresearch.com/industry-analysis/agentic-ai-healthcare-market-report
      - title: AI Agents Market Size And Share | Industry Report, 2033
        url: https://www.grandviewresearch.com/industry-analysis/ai-agents-market-report

  - heading: Market Segmentation by Deployment Type (Cloud vs On-Premise)
    details: |
      Deployment model breakdown shows strong cloud dominance with hybrid architectures gaining momentum:
      
      Cloud: 59.72% of market share in 2025 (Mordor Intelligence)
        - Primary driver: low infrastructure overhead, on-demand scalability, ease of integration
        - Supports AI-as-a-Service models and elastic scaling from dozens to thousands of agents
      
      Hybrid Deployments: Growing at 44.60% CAGR (2026-2031, Mordor Intelligence)
        - Use case: enterprises balancing cloud elasticity with on-premises data sovereignty
        - Key sectors: government, BFSI requiring local inference for compliance
        - Enables zero-trust connectors, unified observability, policy-based routing
      
      On-Premises: Declining relative share but maintained for security-sensitive workflows and data residency requirements
      
      Implication: Hybrid architectures expanding due to enterprise need for data control while capturing cloud scalability benefits, raising integration complexity and service revenue opportunities
    sources:
      - title: Agentic AI Market Share, Size & Growth Outlook to 2031
        url: https://www.mordorintelligence.com/industry-reports/agentic-ai-market

  - heading: Market Segmentation by Function/Use Case
    details: |
      Horizontal use cases (cross-functional applications):
      
      Customer Service & Virtual Assistants: Largest segment in 2025 (MarketsandMarkets), powered by advanced NLP enabling personalized, context-aware interactions
      
      Data Analytics & Business Intelligence: Autonomous agents accelerating data retrieval, dashboarding, and real-time analytics
      
      Finance & Accounting: Autonomous agents reshaping complex workflows, financial forecasting, transaction reconciliation
      
      Workplace Experience: Highest CAGR at 48.7% (2025-2032, MarketsandMarkets), reflecting hybrid/remote work automation demand
      
      Sales: Always-on sales intelligence, deal acceleration, customer insights
      
      Coding & Testing: Autonomous agents boosting developer velocity, software reliability through continuous testing and remediation
      
      Marketing: Precision personalization, campaign autonomy, dynamic content generation
      
      Security Operations: Autonomous threat detection and response, real-time anomaly monitoring
      
      Regulatory Compliance: Continuous monitoring, automated audit trails, compliance automation
      
      Application-Level Segmentation (MarketsandMarkets vertical use cases):
        - Autonomous Process Automation: 23.21% of 2025 deployments (Mordor)
        - Intelligent Virtual Assistants: 44.20% CAGR growth (Mordor, 2026-2031)
        - Predictive Analytics & Decision Support
        - RPA Integration (bridging legacy systems)
        - Smart Manufacturing & IIoT (Industrial Internet of Things integration)
      
      Supply Chain Management: Gartner-specific forecast: SCM software with agentic AI will grow from <$2B (2025) to $53B (2030), reflecting multi-step procurement, vendor management, and logistics optimization
    sources:
      - title: Agentic AI Market Report 2025- 2032, By Offering, Geo, Tech
        url: https://www.marketsandmarkets.com/Market-Reports/agentic-ai-market-208190735.html
      - title: Agentic AI Market Share, Size & Growth Outlook to 2031
        url: https://www.mordorintelligence.com/industry-reports/agentic-ai-market
      - title: Gartner Forecasts Supply Chain Management Software with Agentic AI Will Grow to $53 Billion in Spend by 2030
        url: https://www.supplychain247.com/article/supply-chain-ai-spending-53-billion-2030-gartner

  - heading: Market Segmentation by Component/Offering Type
    details: |
      Component breakdown reveals services-led implementation complexity:
      
      Solutions (Software & Platforms): 61.65% of 2025 revenue (Mordor Intelligence)
        - Agentic AI SaaS: Highest CAGR at 46.8% (2025-2032, MarketsandMarkets), driven by low infrastructure overhead and democratization
        - Agentic AI Development Platforms
        - Agentic AI Orchestration Platforms
        - Agentic AI Process Automation Platforms
        - Agentic AI Enterprise Applications
      
      Services: Growing at 43.80% CAGR (2026-2031, Mordor), outpacing solutions growth
        - Consulting & Integration Services: architecting agent frameworks, legacy system connectors, governance institutionalization
        - Training & Support Services: continuous optimization, behavioral tuning, regulatory audits
        - Implementation scope: data-quality assessment, model governance, employee-acceptance programs
      
      Key Insight: Services growing faster than solutions despite lower initial share, reflecting enterprises' recognition that project success depends on integration depth and change management. Vendors bundling "center-of-excellence in-a-box" offerings with certified ISO-aligned deployments gaining pricing power
    sources:
      - title: Agentic AI Market Share, Size & Growth Outlook to 2031
        url: https://www.mordorintelligence.com/industry-reports/agentic-ai-market
      - title: Agentic AI Market Report 2025- 2032, By Offering, Geo, Tech
        url: https://www.marketsandmarkets.com/Market-Reports/agentic-ai-market-208190735.html

  - heading: Market Segmentation by Organization Size
    details: |
      Enterprise vs SME adoption trajectory:
      
      Large Enterprises: 65.05% market share in 2025 (Mordor Intelligence)
        - Drivers: budget scale, integration capabilities, long-term strategic focus, need for advanced automation and cross-functional decision support
        - Multi-agent research, integration depth, governance investments remain enterprise-led
      
      SMEs (Small & Medium Enterprises): Projected at 43.55% CAGR (2026-2031, Mordor)
        - Growth accelerated by: low-code studios, pay-as-you-go pricing models, pre-trained agents
        - Pre-built agents for customer support, invoice processing, basic analytics enabling deployment within weeks
        - Democratizing access through plug-and-play agent marketplaces and domain-specific agent builders
      
      Implication: SME CAGR (43.55%) approaching large enterprise growth, narrowing adoption gap as accessible frameworks lower entry barriers and tiered SaaS pricing models reduce cost to smaller firms
    sources:
      - title: Agentic AI Market Share, Size & Growth Outlook to 2031
        url: https://www.mordorintelligence.com/industry-reports/agentic-ai-market

  - heading: Market Segmentation by Agent Architecture
    details: |
      Single-Agent vs Multi-Agent Systems:
      
      Single-Agent Systems: 59.24% market share in 2025 (Grand View Research, AI Agents specific report)
        - Faster to implement, lower development/deployment costs
        - Suitable for companies seeking rapid automation without extensive customization
      
      Multi-Agent Systems: 53.30% share in 2025 (Mordor Intelligence, agentic AI specific report)
        - Growing at 43.50% CAGR (2026-2031, Mordor)
        - Drivers: better communication/collaboration, improved coordination, emergent problem-solving capabilities
        - Enable redundancy, specialization, and shared-memory architectures with standardized protocols
        - Enterprise-grade multi-agent deployments showing 90% touchless processing (Siemens example: EUR 5M annual savings)
        - Projected to grow faster than single-agent systems as enterprises decompose monolithic problems into specialized autonomous subsystems
    sources:
      - title: AI Agents Market Size And Share | Industry Report, 2033
        url: https://www.grandviewresearch.com/industry-analysis/ai-agents-market-report
      - title: Agentic AI Market Share, Size & Growth Outlook to 2031
        url: https://www.mordorintelligence.com/industry-reports/agentic-ai-market

  - heading: Regional Breakdown Analysis
    details: |
      Geographic market distribution and growth dynamics:
      
      North America: 39.6%-40.25% market share in 2025 (largest region)
        - Drivers: advanced technological infrastructure, high concentration of leading tech companies, substantial R&D investment
        - Early adoption across defense, healthcare, finance, retail sectors
        - Government initiatives (U.S. National AI Initiative) accelerating research and deployment
        - U.S. market leadership with tech-giant dominance (OpenAI path from $12.7B revenue in 2025 toward $125B by 2029 indicates commercialization momentum)
      
      Europe: Steady growth under stringent regulation (EU AI Act)
        - Benefits: transparent, auditable agent behavior standards elevate governance
        - Leading deployment: Germany (Industry 4.0), UK (fintech compliance), France (sovereign AI stacks)
        - Slower volume but higher governance compliance standards
      
      Asia-Pacific: Fastest-growing region at 44.95% CAGR (2026-2031, Mordor)
        - China: Projected expansion $4.5B (2023) → $82.1B (2033); factory automation and consumer-service bot drivers
        - Japan: 20% annual deployment growth in high-precision manufacturing
        - India: Developer ecosystem supplying global agent design; USD 1.2B government AI mission
        - South Korea & Singapore: smart-city platform integration
        - Patent data: 30% of global AGI filings from China underscores regional technical leadership
      
      Middle East & Africa: Nascent uptake through smart-city, oil-and-gas, public-service rollouts; hybrid architectures addressing bandwidth/latency constraints
      
      Data Point (RAYSolute Consultants, Q1 2026): Total market $7.6B; Asia-Pacific surpassed North America as largest regional market at $3.0B revenue
    sources:
      - title: Agentic AI Market Report 2025- 2032, By Offering, Geo, Tech
        url: https://www.marketsandmarkets.com/Market-Reports/agentic-ai-market-208190735.html
      - title: Agentic AI Market Share, Size & Growth Outlook to 2031
        url: https://www.mordorintelligence.com/industry-reports/agentic-ai-market
      - title: AI Agents Market Size And Share | Industry Report, 2033
        url: https://www.grandviewresearch.com/industry-analysis/ai-agents-market-report

  - heading: Key Demand Drivers
    details: |
      Primary market expansion catalysts identified across research:
      
      Enterprise Hyper-Automation Pressure (+8.2% CAGR impact per Mordor):
        - Labor shortage and efficiency demands driving autonomous, goal-driven agents beyond legacy RPA
        - Executive surveys show 61% of CEOs integrating agents into core operations, surpassing earlier RPA adoption waves
        - Supply-chain studies report 61% faster revenue growth for firms with intelligent automation vs. legacy workflow software
        - Wells Fargo example: loan-processing agents synthesizing multiple data feeds, adapting to compliance updates in real-time, reducing turnaround to minutes
      
      LLM Reasoning Breakthroughs & Maturation (+7.1% CAGR impact per Mordor):
        - Convergence of Large Language Models (LLMs), Robotic Process Automation (RPA), and autonomous tool orchestration
        - Function calling, memory management, orchestration framework reliability improvements enabling multi-step autonomous execution
        - Context-aware, adaptive automation; cross-application task execution without human intervention
        - Models moving from conversational AI to functional autonomy (OpenAI ChatGPT Agent example: integrating Operator + Deep Research for multi-step workflows)
      
      Cloud Infrastructure Maturation & Scalability (+6.5% CAGR impact per Mordor):
        - Serverless inference, GPU-dense instances, AI-tailored container meshes supporting elastic scaling (dozens to thousands of agents)
        - Azure projected $200B revenue by 2028; enterprise data-center outlays for agentic workloads $500K–$1B annually
        - "Agent-as-a-Service" offerings abstracting hardware complexity for mid-market deployment
        - Widespread access to high-performance computing and scalable AI deployment environments reducing capital barriers
      
      Cost Reduction & Operational Efficiency:
        - Manufacturing AI case studies: 15% productivity gains, 10% cost reductions when agentic orchestration replaces manual coordination
        - Autonomous 24/7 operation reducing labor costs and accelerating time-to-decision
      
      Integration with Spatial Computing/XR Ecosystems (+4.3% CAGR impact per Mordor):
        - Agents interpreting 3-D sensor feeds, bridging digital and physical workflows
        - Factory deployment: computer-vision agents monitoring assembly lines, predicting equipment faults, guiding technicians via AR overlays
        - Retail shopper-movement analysis for shelf optimization and real-time recommendations
        - Logistics: autonomous warehouse vehicle coordination reducing collisions and boosting throughput
      
      Emergence of Agentic AI Performance Benchmarks (+3.8% CAGR impact per Mordor):
        - Standardized evaluation metrics for agent reasoning, tool-use efficiency, goal alignment across workflows
        - Addressing enterprise concerns on ROI measurement and safety validation
    sources:
      - title: Agentic AI Market Share, Size & Growth Outlook to 2031
        url: https://www.mordorintelligence.com/industry-reports/agentic-ai-market
      - title: Agentic AI Market Report 2025- 2032, By Offering, Geo, Tech
        url: https://www.marketsandmarkets.com/Market-Reports/agentic-ai-market-208190735.html
      - title: Agentic AI Market Map - Key Players & Startups for 2026
        url: https://adspyder.io/blog/agentic-ai-market-map/

  - heading: Gartner-Specific Spending Forecasts for Agentic AI
    details: |
      Gartner has released targeted agentic AI spending forecasts across specific enterprise domains:
      
      Overall AI Spending Context:
        - Worldwide AI spending forecast: $2.52 trillion in 2026 (44% YoY increase), Jan 2026 Gartner announcement
      
      Supply Chain Management (SCM) Software with Agentic AI:
        - 2025: <$2 billion in agentic AI-specific SCM spend
        - 2030: $53 billion in projected spend (April 2026 Gartner forecast)
        - Growth driver: enterprises moving beyond early testing to production-scale deployments
        - Indicates 50+ CAGR for supply-chain agentic AI subset
      
      Enterprise Application Feature Adoption:
        - Gartner August 2026 prediction: 40% of enterprise apps feature task-specific AI agents by 2026 (up from <5% in 2025)
        - Best-case scenario: agentic AI could drive approximately 30% of enterprise application software revenue by 2030
      
      Organizational AI Investment Patterns:
        - Organizations with successful AI initiatives invest up to 4x more (as % of revenue) in foundational data/analytics areas (April 2026 Gartner)
      
      Note: Gartner separate "AI agents market" (broader autonomy frameworks) from narrow "agentic AI" (enterprise-specific autonomous agents). Grand View Research AI Agents report: $7.63B (2025) → $182.97B (2033) at 49.6% CAGR represents the broader category.

    details: |
      IDC FutureScape 2026 Research on Agentic AI:
      
      Key Predictions:
        - By 2028, 60% of A1000 CIO roles expected to be held by transformational leaders implementing agentic systems
        - IDC projects agentic systems to account for nearly half of all AI spending (across reports and predictions)
        - 45% of organizations expected to orchestrate AI agents at scale by 2030, embedding them across business functions
      
      Strategic Implication: IDC forecasts position agentic AI as dominant segment of broader AI spending, consistent with other analyst projections that agentic systems will command 40%-50% of enterprise AI budgets by 2028-2030

    details: |
      Boston Consulting Group identified a transformative market opportunity in agentic AI services (February 2026):
```

researchers.task:
Research the agentic AI industry value chain structure, identifying where value accrues and margin pools exist. Specifically:
1. Infrastructure/Compute layer: GPU/accelerator providers, cloud infrastructure (inference costs, training costs), who controls bottlenecks
2. Foundation model layer: frontier model providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google DeepMind, Meta), open-source vs proprietary dynamics, model commoditization risk
3. Middleware/Orchestration layer: agent frameworks (LangChain, CrewAI, AutoGen, etc.), observability tools, vector databases, MCP protocol
4. Application layer: vertical SaaS embedding agents, horizontal agent platforms, enterprise workflow automation
5. Where are the durable moats? Data network effects, switching costs, workflow lock-in, distribution advantages
6. What are the emerging monetization models? Per-agent pricing, outcome-based pricing, consumption-based, seat-based
Include specific companies and financial data where available. Focus on 2025-2026 developments.

researchers.findings:
Excellent. Now I have comprehensive data spanning all five layers of the agentic AI value chain, pricing dynamics, moats, market sizing, and key players. Let me compile this into a structured YAML report.

```yaml
topic: "Agentic AI Industry Value Chain Structure: Where Value Accrues and Margin Pools"
summary: "The agentic AI value chain is stratified across five layers with distinct margin dynamics. Compute infrastructure (NVIDIA) commands ~75% gross margins through IP concentration, while foundation models face commoditization pressure from open-source competitors, forcing a shift toward enterprise LLM market share (Anthropic now 40% vs. OpenAI's 27%). The middleware/orchestration layer (LangChain, CrewAI, AutoGen) is consolidating via open-source adoption and integration with hyperscalers, while the application layer is rapidly monetizing through outcome-based and per-action pricing models rather than traditional seat licensing. Durable moats are forming around data network effects, workflow lock-in, and vertical SaaS integration rather than model capability alone."

findings:
  - heading: "Infrastructure/Compute Layer: NVIDIA Dominance and Margin Concentration"
    details: |
      NVIDIA controls the highest-margin layer of the value chain with ~75% gross margins (FY2026: $215.9B revenue, 75.0-75.2% GAAP/non-GAAP gross margins). The company captured 54% market share of AI accelerators globally in 2025, with Huawei at 28% in China. GPU supply remains constrained with 36-52 week lead times for data center GPUs and hyperscaler capacity constraints persist into Q1-Q2 2026.
      
      Key bottlenecks:
      - H100 and H200 GPUs dominate training infrastructure; H200 offers 1.4x memory bandwidth improvement (4.8TB/s vs 3.35TB/s) but identical raw compute (1,979 TFLOPS)
      - NVIDIA's B200 Blackwell generation is entering deployment phase with improved specs, exacerbating allocation challenges
      - Inference cost compression occurs at ~10x annually (December 2025 update), driven by hardware advances and software optimization, but absolute scale requirements remain steep
      - AI infrastructure spending forecast: $530B from Big Tech in 2026 alone
      
      Margin compression threatens downstream players: AI APIs average ~52% gross margins because inference requires continuous GPU compute with every query—marginal cost scales directly with usage volume. OpenAI projects 48% gross profit margin in 2025, improving to 70% by 2029 only if infrastructure leverage improves.
    sources:
      - title: "NVIDIA Announces Financial Results for Fourth Quarter and Fiscal 2026"
        url: "https://nvidianews.nvidia.com/news/nvidia-announces-financial-results-for-fourth-quarter-and-fiscal-2026"
      - title: "GPU Shortage 2026: How to Secure AI Compute When GPUs Are Sold Out"
        url: "https://www.spheron.network/blog/gpu-shortage-2026/"
      - title: "The State Of AI Infrastructure: Demand, Costs, And Custom Silicon"
        url: "https://www.ark-invest.com/articles/analyst-research/the-state-of-ai-infrastructure-demand-costs-custom-silicon"
      - title: "Inference Unit Economics: The True Cost Per Million Tokens"
        url: "https://introl.com/blog/inference-unit-economics-true-cost-per-million-tokens-guide"
      - title: "The AI Inference Market in 2025 — Hardware Consolidation, Pricing Wars"
        url: "https://www.softwareseni.com/the-ai-inference-market-in-2025-hardware-consolidation-pricing-wars-and-what-it-means-for-buyers/"

  - heading: "Foundation Model Layer: Rapid Commoditization and Market Consolidation"
    details: |
      The proprietary frontier model market is experiencing sharp consolidation driven by three dynamics: (1) enterprise spending shift toward Anthropic, (2) open-source model quality convergence, and (3) aggressive pricing competition.
      
      Enterprise LLM market share (per Menlo Ventures 2025 data):
      - Anthropic: 40% of enterprise LLM spend (up from 24% in 2024, 12% in 2023)
      - OpenAI: 27% (down from 50% in 2023)
      - Google/Other: 33% combined
      - Enterprise spend hit $8.4B mid-2025 (up from $3.5B six months prior), but switching rates remain low (11% of teams switched providers, consolidating to winners)
      
      Pricing dynamics (January 2026):
      - OpenAI GPT-4o: $2.50 input / $10.00 output per 1M tokens; GPT-5.4 mini: $0.15 input / $0.60 output
      - Anthropic Claude Opus 4.7: $5.00 input / $25.00 output per 1M tokens (67% reduction from Opus 4.0/4.1 at $15/$75)
      - Anthropic Claude Haiku: $0.25 input / $1.25 output per 1M tokens
      - DeepSeek (open-source): Free or near-free for open models; API pricing 10-50x cheaper than OpenAI due to Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) architecture
      
      Model economics:
      - Average duration of human task a model can reliably do is doubling every 7 months
      - Open-source models (Meta Llama 4 in April 2025 with 15 trillion tokens training data, 7x more than Llama 2; DeepSeek V3.2; Mistral) are closing capability gap with proprietary models
      - Anthropic revenue trajectory: ~$1B annualized at end-2024 → $5B by August 2025 → $9B by end-2025
      - Pricing model economics "confusing" per Innovation Endeavors State of Foundation Models 2025 report—high training costs with compressed inference pricing leaves value concentrated at hyperscale deployment
    sources:
      - title: "2025: The State of Generative AI in the Enterprise (Menlo Ventures)"
        url: "https://menlovc.com/perspective/2025-the-state-of-generative-ai-in-the-enterprise/"
      - title: "The AI Race Has Split in Two — and Anthropic Is Leading One Half"
        url: "https://medium.com/@Micheal-Lanham/the-ai-race-has-split-in-two-and-anthropic-is-leading-one-half-dca65d4ef3a1"
      - title: "OpenAI Statistics 2026: Users, Revenue & Market Share"
        url: "https://www.getpanto.ai/blog/openai-statistics"
      - title: "GPT-4o Pricing Per Million Tokens: Complete Cost Guide"
        url: "https://www.aifreeapi.com/en/posts/gpt-4o-pricing-per-million-tokens"
      - title: "Anthropic API Pricing in 2026: Complete Guide"
        url: "https://www.finout.io/blog/anthropic-api-pricing"
      - title: "DeepSeek-V3.2 Matches GPT-5 at 10x Lower Cost"
        url: "https://introl.com/blog/deepseek-v3-2-open-source-ai-cost-advantage"
      - title: "State of Foundation Models - 2025 (Innovation Endeavors)"
        url: "https://foundationmodelreport.ai/2025.pdf"

  - heading: "Middleware/Orchestration Layer: Open-Source Consolidation with Proprietary Monetization Paths"
    details: |
      The agent framework market consolidated from 30+ competitors (2024) to 5 major players by end-2025, with clear separation between open-source (developer-adopted) and enterprise SaaS positioning.
      
      Leading frameworks and funding/valuations (Oct-Nov 2025):
      - LangChain: $1.25B valuation (Series B, $125M raised Oct 2025, led by IVP, CapitalG, Sapphire Ventures). Hybrid model: open-source framework + LangSmith enterprise platform. Developer adoption: Klarna, Rippling, major enterprises
      - CrewAI: $24.5M total funding; Series A ($18M led by Insight Partners Oct 2024); estimated $76M valuation (Q3 2024); ~$2.4-3.2M estimated 2025 revenue
      - AutoGen (Microsoft): Merged with Semantic Kernel into Microsoft Agent Framework (October 2025); production-ready 1.0 targeted for Q1 2026 GA; combines AutoGen orchestration with enterprise .NET/Python support
      - Haystack, MetaGPT, Ray framework—each capturing vertical niches (search/RAG, task planning, distributed compute respectively)
      
      Adoption patterns:
      - 40% of Fortune 500 companies now use open-source AI agents
      - Large enterprises leading adoption: 57.3% have agents running in production, 30.4% in pilots (LangChain State of Agent Engineering)
      - Gartner projects 40% of enterprise apps will feature task-specific AI agents by 2026 (up from <5% in 2025)
      
      Monetization paths emerging:
      - LangChain: Platform fees for observability, monitoring, deployment (LangSmith subscription model)
      - CrewAI: Moving from open-source to managed SaaS platform (CrewAI Studio) with usage-based billing
      - Microsoft AutoGen: Integrated into Azure ecosystem with consumption-based pricing (Azure compute billing)
      - Vector databases (Pinecone, Weaviate, Milvus) integrate directly via Model Context Protocol (MCP)
      
      Model Context Protocol (MCP) emergence:
      - MCP standard enables standardized agent-to-application communication (Anthropic-led initiative, adopted by OpenAI, Google, major frameworks)
      - Direct integrations with Pinecone, Weaviate, ChromaDB for vector storage and retrieval
      - MCP servers act as middleware layer between agents and external systems—potential monetization point
    sources:

        url: "https://sparkco.ai/blog/deep-dive-into-vector-databases-for-ai-agents"

  - heading: "Vector Database & Observability Middleware: Emerging Margin Pools"
    details: |
      Vector databases are becoming critical infrastructure for agent memory, retrieval-augmented generation (RAG), and semantic search. Pinecone is the clear market leader by adoption but faces competition from open-source alternatives and cloud-native solutions.
      
      Pinecone metrics (Oct 2025):
      - Series C: $100M raised at $750M valuation (led by Andreessen Horowitz, ICONIQ)
      - Total funding: $138M
      - Estimated 2025 revenue: $14M from ~4K customers
      - Market context: Vector database market reached $4.3B in 2025, projected to reach $14B by 2030
      
      Competitive alternatives:
      - Weaviate, Milvus, Qdrant, ChromaDB (open-source)
      - MongoDB Atlas Vector Search (integrating vector search into existing document DB)
      - Pinecone's competitive advantage: MCP integration, managed infrastructure, enterprise features (pod-based pricing, hybrid search)
      
      Observability layer (AI-specific monitoring):
      - Datadog commands 51.82% market share in data center observability (broader market: $62.9B by 2025)
      - AI observability specifically: Datadog, Arize AI, WhyLabs, TensorBoard, LangSmith (LangChain's observability platform)
      - Datadog's specialized AI observability capabilities enabling disproportionate market share capture
      - Financial services alone spending $80B+ on AI in 2025; observability tied to compliance and performance optimization

    details: |
      The application layer shows sharp divergence between vertical SaaS (domain-specific agents embedded in CRM, ERP, logistics, helpdesk) and horizontal agent platforms. Vertical SaaS is outpacing horizontal platforms at nearly 2x growth rate in 2025-2026.
      
      Market sizing:
      - Global agentic AI market: $6.96-9.1B in 2025, projected $9.89B-10.9B in 2026
      - By 2030-2034: $52.62B-182.97B depending on analyst (ranges: 40.5%-49.6% CAGR)
      - Vertical AI agents segment: Highest CAGR at 62.7% (2025-2030 forecast)
      
      Key players and monetization:
      
      Salesforce Agentforce (Enterprise CRM):
      - Multiple pricing models rolled out (as of March 2026):
        * Pay-per-conversation: $2.00 per conversation (initial 2024 model)
        * Flex Credits: $0.10 per action (May 2025 update)
        * Per-user licensing: $125-650/user/month (March 2026 update, depending on industry add-ons)
      - Requires Enterprise ($165/user/month minimum) or Unlimited Edition ($330/user/month)
      - Outcome of pivoting pricing models 3+ times in 18 months reflects market discovery of what customers will pay
      
      Zendesk AI Agents:
      - Outcome-based pricing leader: $1.50 per automated resolution (successful ticket close)

      Pricing model evolution:
      - Traditional seat-based pricing compressing: SaaS companies without outcome-based pricing reported 90% seat count reduction when agents deployed
      - Usage-based pricing majority among new AI SaaS launches (2026): consumption metrics tied to actions, resolutions, or workflows completed
      - Hybrid models emerging: Floor per-user + outcome upside (e.g., Salesforce's multi-model approach)

        url: "https://www.zendesk.com/blog/ai/agentic-ai/outcome-based-pricing/"

      - title: "SaaS Pricing Is Breaking: Why Per-Seat Models Don't Survive the AI Era"
        url: "https://www.mindstudio.ai/blog/saas-pricing-ai-agent-era/"

  - heading: "Durable Moats: Data Lock-In, Workflow Stickiness, and Vertical Integration Over Model Capability"
    details: |
      Traditional AI moats (proprietary data, model quality) are eroding as open-source models converge and inference costs commoditize. Emerging moats cluster around three mechanisms:
      
      1. Workflow Lock-In and Switching Costs (Moderating):
      - Historical SaaS switching costs (implementation complexity, workflow integration) are weakening as AI agents reduce friction
      - However, data lock-in and multi-year operational history create stickiness in vertical SaaS
      - Integration breadth (CRM-to-ERP, helpdesk-to-billing) creates operational switching costs even if model quality equalized
      - Example: Salesforce Agentforce pricing churn (3 model changes in 18 months) shows early-stage market but indicates customers sticking through pivots
      
      2. Data Network Effects and Proprietary Datasets:
      - Vertical SaaS carrying multiple years of proprietary customer data, transaction history, domain-specific patterns
      - Every agent execution generates new training signals, behavioral data, edge cases
      - Competitive advantage accumulates over time: more agents → more data → better task-specific model fine-tuning → lower inference cost per action
      - Data flywheel is permanent: competitor cannot replicate customer's operational dataset
      - Example: Zendesk resolution data, Salesforce CRM customer interaction patterns, UiPath RPA execution logs—competitors cannot acquire these datasets
      
      3. Distribution & Integration Advantage:
      - Integration breadth into enterprise systems (CRM, ERP, HRIS, accounting) acts as distribution moat
      - Embedding agents into workflow creates multi-product stickiness (vertical SaaS advantage over horizontal platforms)
      - "The integration becomes the moat: Model quality is converging, so what matters is owning the surface where work happens" (per AI Pioneers, Feb 2026)
      - Example: Anthropic's Claude in Slack/Claude desktop creates distribution advantages despite OpenAI having API maturity
      
      4. Observability and Governance Moat (Emerging):
      - Compliance, audit trails, explainability requirements create switching costs for enterprise agents
      - Datadog, enterprise observability vendors capturing 2-3x pricing multiples by providing governance layer atop agents
      - Regulatory requirements (financial services, healthcare) will deepen observability lock-in
      
      Challenged Moats:
      - Model capability/quality: DeepSeek's 10x cheaper inference with comparable quality erodes proprietary model pricing power
      - Prompts portability: "The wave of AI agent churn to come: Prompts are portable" (SaaStr Apr 2026)—easy to switch if no data network effects
      - Open-source adoption: 40% of Fortune 500 using open-source AI agents reduces vendor lock-in
    sources:

        url: "https://travismay.medium.com/moats-matter-again-a6359b0d31da"

  - heading: "Monetization Models: Shift from Seat-Based to Outcome-Based and Per-Action Pricing"
    details: |
      SaaS pricing is undergoing radical restructuring in the agentic era. Gartner projected 30% of enterprise SaaS solutions incorporating outcome-based pricing by 2025 (up from 15% in 2022).
      
      Evolving models (2025-2026):
      
      1. Outcome-Based (Value-Aligned):
      - Zendesk model (market leader): $1.50 per resolved ticket / $2.00 pay-as-you-go
      - Sierra, Kore.ai, other customer service agents following
      - Aligns vendor incentives with customer ROI
      - Challenges: Difficult to measure "outcome" consistently; risk transfer to vendor; requires vendor to influence outcome reliably
      
      2. Per-Action / Per-Workflow (Consumption-Based):
      - Salesforce Agentforce Flex Credits: $0.10 per action (May 2025 update)
      - This model gained traction after per-conversation pricing proved too lumpy
      - Separates agent invocation frequency from business outcome—intermediate moat
      - Scales with volume but decoupled from customer ROI
      
      3. Hybrid Per-User + Outcome (Emerging):
      - Salesforce's current model (March 2026): $125-650/user/month + flex credits for overages
      - Creates floor revenue + upside sharing
      - Attempts to capture both adoption (user seats) and consumption (actions)
      
      4. Seat-Based (Declining):
      - Traditional enterprise SaaS model ($50-150/user/month)
      - Failing in agent context: agents reduce manual work, compress seat counts
      - SaaS companies without outcome-based pricing seeing 90% seat count compression when agents deployed—revenue cliff
      
      5. Batch/On-Demand Inference (Infrastructure):
      - AWS Bedrock batch inference: 50% discount vs. on-demand (for large-scale asynchronous processing)
      - Token-based pricing (OpenAI, Anthropic, AWS models): $/1M tokens input/output
      - Reflects underlying GPU infrastructure cost structure
      
      Price discovery still in flux:
      - Salesforce iterated 3+ times in 18 months (conversation → flex credits → per-user), signaling no consensus model yet
      - Vertical SaaS companies experimenting: customer service agents (outcome-based), manufacturing workflow agents (per-workflow), enterprise RPA (per-execution)
      - Adoption challenge: Outcome-based requires vendor confidence in agent quality; many companies reverting to hybrid models to derisk

  - heading: "Cloud Infrastructure Layer: AWS, Azure, Google Competing on API Pricing and Managed Services"
    details: |
      Hyperscalers (AWS, Microsoft Azure, Google Cloud) are positioning themselves as foundation model distribution and inference infrastructure providers. Competitive dynamics on pricing and integration depth are emerging.
      
      Pricing tiers (Jan-Apr 2026):
      - AWS Bedrock: Offers Claude, Llama, Mistral, DeepSeek models; batch inference 50% discount; provisioned throughput options for committed volume
      - Azure OpenAI Service: Exclusive enterprise license for OpenAI GPT-4o; GPT-4.1-2025-04-14: $2.00 input / $0.50 cached input per 1M tokens; batch API returns results within 24 hours
      - Google Vertex AI: Multiple model access (Google's Gemini, Anthropic Claude via partnership, Meta Llama)
      - Case example (2025): Fortune 500 retail overspent $2.3M annually on Azure OpenAI vs. AWS Bedrock equivalents due to lack of benchmarking
      
      Integration advantage:
      - Azure: Tight integration with Microsoft 365, Copilot stack, enterprise licensing
      - AWS: Broad service portfolio (SageMaker for fine-tuning, Lambda for serverless inference, RDS for databases)
      - Google: Data warehouse/analytics integration (BigQuery)
      
      Observability and enterprise features:
      - Hyperscalers bundling AI observability into base offerings (Datadog competitive threat)
      - Cost optimization tools (AWS Compute Optimizer, Azure Cost Management) highlighting hyperscaler lock-in
    sources:

  - heading: "Market Adoption and Enterprise Readiness Signals"
    details: |
      Enterprise adoption is accelerating rapidly across verticals:
      
      Production deployment status (LangChain survey 2025):
      - 57.3% of large enterprises have agents running in production
      - 30.4% have agents in pilots/POC stage
      - <13% still evaluating or have no agents deployed
      
      Vertical adoption momentum:
      - Gartner (Aug 2025): 40% of enterprise apps will feature task-specific AI agents by 2026 (up from <5% in 2025)
      - Manufacturing: 4x adoption acceleration (Deloitte): 6% (2025) → 24% (2026)
      - Financial services: $80B+ AI spending in 2025, heavily weighted to agent automation
      - ITSM/IT Operations: Agentic AI adoption in ITSM workflows accelerating, replacing workflow automation RPA layer
      
      Survey evidence:
      - McKinsey Global Survey 2025: Enterprises moving from AI pilots to production; integration with business processes is bottleneck
      - MIT Sloan (Nov 2025): 66% of organizations with extensive agentic AI adoption expect operating model changes vs. 42% with limited adoption
      - PwC AI Agent Survey (May 2025): Executives prioritizing agent deployment in customer service, supply chain, and finance
      
      Technology penetration curve indicators:
      - Open-source frameworks adoption: 40% of Fortune 500 using open-source agents (vs. proprietary SaaS)
      - Multi-agent systems majority: 72% multi-agent adoption in a single year (per AI Agent Observability report)
      - Integration breadth: Enterprises deploying agents across 3+ domains (workflows) simultaneously
```

```yaml
topic: "Agentic AI Ecosystem: Key Companies & Market Mapping (2025-2026)"
summary: "The agentic AI space is experiencing explosive growth, with 2025 marking a pivotal year across the full value chain. Foundation model companies (Anthropic, OpenAI, xAI) are reaching unprecedented valuations ($183B-$380B), while pure-play agent companies (Cognition/Devin, Sierra, Parloa) have hit unicorn status at scale. Enterprise platforms (Salesforce, ServiceNow) are rapidly monetizing agents, and the overall agentic AI market is projected to reach $93-139B by 2032 from a $7B base in 2025."

findings:
  - heading: "Market Size & Growth"
    details: |
      The agentic AI market is expanding at a 40-46% CAGR:
      • 2025 Market Size: $6.96-7.29 billion (multiple sources)
      • 2032 Projected: $93.20-139.19 billion
      • CAGR: 40.5%-44.6% (2025-2032)
      
      AI startups raised $73.1 billion in Q4 2025 alone, representing 58% of all global venture funding that quarter. Q4 2025 global AI funding reached $54 billion, with annual 2025 total of $211 billion (85% YoY increase).

  - heading: "1. Hyperscalers/Infrastructure"
    details: |
      NVIDIA (NASDAQ: NVDA):
      • Q4 FY2026 (Feb 2026): Record revenue $68.1B (+20% QoQ, +73% YoY)
      • Data Center revenue: $62.3B (+24% QoQ)
      • Q4 FY2025 (Feb 2025): $39.3B revenue (+12% QoQ, +78% YoY)
      • Data center revenue reached $62B in Q4 2026 (up from ~$35.6B in Q4 2025)
      • Controls 86% of AI data center GPU market as of late 2025
      • Market cap exceeded $3 trillion in 2025
      
      Microsoft (NASDAQ: MSFT):
      • FY2025 Q4: Revenue $76.4B (+18% YoY); Copilot/AI driving growth
      • Azure full-year revenue exceeded $75B (annual run rate), growing at 35-39% YoY
      • Invested heavily in OpenAI partnership; deployed Copilot across Microsoft 365
      • FY2026 Q2: $81.3B revenue (+17% YoY)
      • Market cap exceeded $2 trillion in 2025
      
      Google/Alphabet (NASDAQ: GOOGL):
      • Q4 2025: Total annual revenues exceeded $400B for first time
      • Google Cloud: $17.66B in Q4 2025 revenue (+48% growth)
      • Annual Cloud run rate: $70B+
      • Google Search revenue: +17% YoY
      • Gemini Enterprise reached 8 million subscribers; API calls doubled to 85 billion by August 2025
      • Market cap exceeded $2 trillion in 2025
      
      Amazon/AWS (NASDAQ: AMZN):
      • Q4 2025: AWS revenue $128.7B (+20% YoY), Full-year 2025 revenue $716.9B (+12% YoY)
      • AWS operating income: $12.5B with 35% operating margin
      • Bedrock customer spend: +60% sequentially
      • Rufus AI agent enabled over 300M interactions
      • Market cap exceeded $2 trillion in 2025
      
      Meta (NASDAQ: META):
      • Llama 3.1: 275M downloads by Q4 2025, controlling 65-70% of open-source LLM market
      • Building internal agentic AI agents for advertising infrastructure
      • Launched Superintelligence team (MSL) mid-2025
      • Investment in agentic AI infrastructure for MetaAI assistant (~600M MAU)
      • Market cap exceeded $2 trillion in 2025

  - heading: "2. Foundation Model Companies"
    details: |
      OpenAI:
      • Valuation: $850B-$852B post-money (Feb 2026, after $122B capital raise)
      • ARR: $12.2B as of July 2025, up from $6B in January 2025 (doubled in 7 months)
      • Codex: Launched May 2025, AI coding agent with concurrent task execution; hit 3M weekly active users by April 2026
      • Operator: Browser agent launched January 2025 for repetitive tasks
      • Total funding: $60B+ raised through early 2026
      
      Anthropic:
      • Valuation: $380B post-money (Feb 2026, Series G at $30B)
      • Revenue run-rate: $14B (Feb 2026), with 10x annual growth for three consecutive years
      • Series F (Sept 2025): $13B at $183B valuation, led by ICONIQ
      • Claude ARR: $1.2B in 2025 (+1000% YoY)
      • Over 1,000 enterprise customers spending $1M+/year on Claude
      • 50%+ of revenue from enterprise customers
      • Top customers (Cursor, GitHub Copilot) = 25% of mid-2025 revenue
      
      xAI:
      • Valuation: $250B+ (recent funding rounds) vs. $50B Series C (Dec 2024)
      • Series E: $20B (Jan 2026) - upsized from initial rounds
      • July 2025: $10B raise ($5B debt + $5B equity)
      • Grok: Revenue estimated at $100M annualized (Dec 2024); evolved through 4 major versions
      • Heavy backed by Elon Musk; integrated with X (Twitter)
      
      Mistral AI:
      • Valuation: $14B (Sept 2025) vs. $13.3B (mid-2025)
      • Revenue: $100M run-rate (2025); annualized contracts ~$325M as of Sept 2025
      • Series funding: $3B raised total
      • Sept 2025: $2B raise led by ASML at $14B valuation
      • Positioned as open-weight alternative to proprietary models
      
      Cohere:
      • Valuation: $7B (Sept 2025) vs. $6.8B (Aug 2025)
      • August 2025: $500M Series raise at $6.8B
      • September 2025: Additional $100M at $7B valuation
      • Total funding: $1.5B raised
      • Focus on enterprise AI models
      
      Google DeepMind:
      • Gemini as primary foundation model family
      • Gemini Enterprise: 8M subscribers, 85B API calls by Aug 2025
      • Integrated into Google Cloud and Workspace
      • No separate valuation; parent company (Alphabet) valued $2T+
      
      DeepSeek (China):
      • Valuation: $10B target (April 2026, seeking $300M external funding first time)
      • Previously estimated at $3.4B in 2025
      • R1 reasoning model: Launched Jan 2025, matched OpenAI's top model on benchmarks for ~$6M training cost
      • Demonstrates efficiency alternative model

  - heading: "3. Enterprise AI Platforms"
    details: |
      Salesforce (NYSE: CRM) - Agentforce:
      • FY2025 Q4: Revenue $37.9B (+8.73% YoY); $12.43B free cash flow
      • Agentforce 360 Platform/Slack revenue: +37% growth in Q4
      • Agentforce launched Oct 2024; Agentforce 2dx (March 2025) enabled agent-to-agent collaboration
      • 180 organizations replaced legacy IT support tools with Agentforce
      • FY2026 Q4 (March 2026): $11.2B revenue (+12% YoY)
      
      ServiceNow (NYSE: NOW):
      • Q4 2025: Subscription revenue $3.47B (+21% YoY), Total revenue $3.6B (+20.66% YoY)
      • AI agents positioned as orchestration layer for business processes
      • Multiple agents working as subject-matter experts (SMEs)
      • CMDB integration for IT service management agents
      
      SAP:
      • RISE with SAP & Datasphere: Core platform offerings
      • Joule (AI assistant) integrated with SAP Cloud ERP solutions
      • Change Record Management Agent available in SAP Cloud ERP
      • Business AI & Agent Framework announced for Q4 2025
      • Focus on sovereign cloud offerings and agent partnerships
      
      IBM (NYSE: IBM):
      • Q4 2025: Revenue $19.67B (+12.8% YoY); Full-year $67.5B (+8% YoY)
      • GenAI book of business: $12.5B by end of 2025 (doubled during year)
      • watsonx: Core AI/agent platform; "book of business roughly doubled" per CEO
      • Record $14.7B free cash flow in 2025
      • Software now ~45% of IBM business
      
      Palantir Technologies (NYSE: PLTR):
      • Q4 2025: Revenue $1.407B (+70% YoY, highest growth as public company)
      • U.S. revenue: +93% YoY to $1.076B
      • Full-year 2025: $4.475B revenue (+56% YoY)
      • GAAP net income: $1.63B; adjusted FCF: $2.27B
      • Positioned as enterprise data & decision platform; agents for data integration

  - heading: "4. Pure-Play Agent Companies"
    details: |
      Cognition Labs - Devin (AI Coding Agent):
      • Valuation: $10.2B (Sept 2025) vs. $4B (early 2025)
      • Series funding: $400M+ led by Founders Fund
      • Devin ARR: $73M (June 2025)
      • Product: Autonomous software engineer; merged hundreds of thousands of PRs
      • Status: Clear leader in AI coding agent category
      
      Sierra (Bret Taylor's Customer Service Agent):
      • Valuation: $10B (Sept 2025)
      • Series funding: $350M in Sept 2025
      • ARR: $100M (Dec 2025)
      • Positioned as customer experience/service AI agent
      • Built by Bret Taylor (ex-Salesforce), backed by Greenoaks Capital
      
      Parloa (Germany-based, Enterprise CX Agents):
      • Valuation: $3B (Jan 2026) - tripled in 8 months
      • Series D: $350M (Jan 2026) led by General Catalyst
      • Founded: 2019 (6 years old)
      • Revenue: $50M+ run-rate (Dec 2025)
      • 200+ engineers; serves financial services & Fortune 500 companies
      • European leader in agentic AI lending automation
      
      Adept AI (Workflow Automation):
      • Valuation: $1B (2024)
      • Series B: $350M (2024)
      • Total funding: $415M
      • Focus: Manual, repetitive workflow automation
      • Employees: 60-120
      
      Moveworks (IT Service Agent):
      • Acquired by ServiceNow (March 2025) for $2.85B
      • Previously valued at $2.1B (Series C, June 2021)
      • Raised $315M total funding
      • Provided AI agent for IT/HR service requests
      
      UiPath (NYSE: PATH) - RPA/Agent Platform:
      • Current valuation: $6.2B (market cap at ~$11.50 stock price, March 2026)
      • IPO (2021): Targeted ~$26B valuation
      • Q4 FY2026: Strong operational performance
      • Transitioning legacy RPA platform to agentic AI; valuation normalized from 50x ARR peak
      • ScreenPlay (UI Agent): Entered public preview (July 2025)
      
      Automation Anywhere (RPA/Agent Platform):
      • Valuation: ~$10B (latest estimate, still private)
      • Total funding: $1.1B over 8 rounds (as of Oct 2022)
      • Focus: Large-enterprise AI agents for automation
      • CEO highlighted significant customer savings from AI agents (late 2025)
      • Pre-IPO status; merger discussions with C3.AI mentioned
      
      Cursor (AI IDE/Coding Agent):
      • Valuation: $50B (March 2026, raising $2B)
      • ARR: $500M+ (Jan 2026)
      • Built by 4 MIT students; overtook GitHub Copilot as #1 AI developer tool
      • Pro plan: $20/month
      • Significantly outperforms competitors in benchmarks (2 vs. 3 vs. 5 rounds for complex tasks)

  - heading: "5. Developer Tools/Middleware"
    details: |
      LangChain:
      • Status: Leading AI agent framework
      • 6 core modules: model I/O, external data, chain concept, memory, agents, callbacks
      • Widely integrated with LangGraph for orchestration
      • Open-source; community-driven development
      • No separate valuation disclosed; backed by strategic partners
      
      Databricks:
      • Valuation: $134B (Dec 2025) after Series L
      • Series L: $4B+ funding (Dec 2025)
      • Previously Series K: $1B at >$100B valuation (Sept 2025)
      • Revenue: $4B+ run-rate (Sept 2025)
      • Platform for data intelligence and AI agents
      
      n8n (Workflow/Agent Orchestration):
      • Valuation: $2.5B (Oct 2025)
      • Series C: $180M (Oct 2025)
      • Total funding: $240M
      • Enables AI agent workflow automation
      
      Dify (Low-Code Agent Builder):
      • Series Pre-A: $30M (March 2026)
      • Platform for building agentic AI workflows
      • Open-source components
      
      Weights & Biases (ML Experiment Management):
      • Leading platform for model training, evaluation, agentic AI experiments
      • Used by Cloudera, Datadog, Dataiku, DataRobot, DataStax
      • Valuation/revenue not publicly disclosed; Series funding active through 2025
      
      Hugging Face:
      • Valuation: $7B (Aug 2025) after $500M raise vs. $4.5B (earlier 2025)
      • August 2025 raise: $500M at ~$7B valuation
      • Total funding: $400M+ over multiple rounds
      • 13M users, 2M+ public models, 500k+ datasets as of March 2026
      • Key infrastructure for open-source agents
      
      Retool (Low-Code Application Builder):
      • Valuation: $3.2B (July 2025)
      • Series C: $200M (July 2025)
      • Agents support in low-code platform
      • 48% of non-engineers now building production-ready solutions
      
      FlowiseAI:
      • Acquired by Workday (2025) for undisclosed sum
      • Previously funding round: $100M+ Series rounds

  - heading: "Market Dynamics & Competitive Positioning"
    details: |
      Funding & Valuation Trends (2025):
      • Average agentic AI round size: $51M (up from $37M in 2024)
      • Q4 2025 saw 5 of top 10 largest VC rounds globally go to AI companies
      • Sequoia Capital & a16z dominate unicorn backing; a16z raised record $15B in 2025
      • Series funding rounds average upward trend, indicating market maturation
      
      Hyperscaler Dominance:
      • NVIDIA maintains 86% GPU market share for AI (late 2025)
      • Microsoft, Google, Amazon leveraging cloud scale & API infrastructure
      • All 5 hyperscalers now have comprehensive agent/agentic offerings
      • Meta's Llama open-source achieving significant adoption (275M downloads)
      
      Foundation Model Consolidation:
      • OpenAI: $850B+ valuation, $12.2B ARR (fastest scaling SaaS ever)
      • Anthropic: $380B valuation, $14B ARR (Feb 2026), closing gap with OpenAI
      • xAI: $250B+ valuation, rapidly scaling investment
      • Mistral: $14B valuation, European challenger
      • Gap widening between tier-1 (OpenAI, Anthropic, xAI) and tier-2 (Mistral, Cohere)
      
      Enterprise Agent Winners:
      • Parloa: $3B valuation, $50M+ ARR (customer service focus)
      • Cognition/Devin: $10.2B valuation, $73M ARR (software engineering focus)
      • Sierra: $10B valuation, $100M ARR (enterprise CX focus)
      • Agentforce (Salesforce): 37% revenue growth in Q4; 180 IT orgs adopting
      • All showing clear monetization paths and rapid enterprise adoption
      
      Developer Tools Consolidation:
      • Databricks: $134B valuation, leading data+AI infrastructure
      • Hugging Face: $7B valuation, open-source model hub dominance
      • LangChain: De facto standard for agent frameworks
      • Low-code platforms (Retool, n8n) achieving $2-3B+ valuations
      
      Key Differentiation Factors:
      • Reasoning/inference quality (R1 vs GPT-4 benchmarks)
      • Training efficiency (DeepSeek $6M vs traditional billions)
      • Domain specialization (Devin for coding, Parloa for CX, Palantir for data)
      • Enterprise compliance & governance (IBM watsonx, SAP)
      • Open-source accessibility (Llama, Mistral, Hugging Face)
```

researchers.findings:
Excellent. Now I have comprehensive data. Let me compile the findings into a structured YAML report:

```yaml
topic: "Enterprise Agentic AI Adoption Trends 2025-2026"
summary: "Enterprise agentic AI adoption has accelerated dramatically in 2025-2026, with 23-52% of organizations actively deploying or scaling agents, depending on the survey. While early adopters report strong ROI (171% average, up to 192% for U.S. enterprises), over 40% of projects face cancellation risk by 2027 due to escalating costs, unclear business value, and security governance gaps. Key industries—BFSI, retail, and manufacturing—account for 70% of use cases, with customer service, contract automation, and supply chain optimization leading adoption."

findings:
  - heading: "Adoption Rates & Deployment Scale"
    details: |
      McKinsey's 2025 global survey (published November 2025) found 23% of organizations are actively scaling agentic AI systems in at least one business function, with 39% in experimental/pilot phases. 
      
      Google Cloud's 2025 ROI study (September 2025, surveying 3,466+ executives) reports 52% of organizations have deployed AI agents in production, though among GenAI-using organizations specifically, 52% of executives report agents already in production.
      
      Gartner predicts 40% of enterprise applications will integrate task-specific AI agents by end of 2026 (up from <5% in 2025)—an 8x increase in 9 months.
      
      PwC data shows 79% of organizations have adopted AI agents at some level by 2025, but governance lags severely: 86% of agents deployed without security approval (Gravitee data, 2026).
      
      KPMG Q3 2025 AI Pulse Survey: 57% of organizations favor a blended build-and-buy approach (up from 51% earlier in 2025), reflecting shift from pure build-in-house model. Enterprise generative AI spending reached $37 billion in 2025 (3.2x from 2024), with 76% purchased vs. 24% built internally—a complete reversal from 2024 (47% built/53% purchased).
    sources:
      - title: "McKinsey State of AI 2025 Global Survey"
        url: "https://www.mckinsey.com/capabilities/quantumblack/our-insights/the-state-of-ai"
      - title: "Google Cloud ROI of AI Study September 2025"
        url: "https://cloud.google.com/transform/roi-of-ai-how-agents-help-business"
      - title: "Gartner: 40% of Enterprise Apps Will Feature Task-Specific AI Agents by 2026"
        url: "https://www.gartner.com/en/newsroom/press-releases/2025-08-26-gartner-predicts-40-percent-of-enterprise-apps-will-feature-task-specific-ai-agents-by-2026-up-from-less-than-5-percent-in-2025"
      - title: "Beam AI: Build vs Buy—76% of Enterprises Purchased in 2025"
        url: "https://beam.ai/agentic-insights/the-great-ai-flip-why-76-of-enterprises-stopped-building-ai-in-house"
      - title: "KPMG Agentic AI Untangled: Q3 2025 AI Pulse Survey"
        url: "https://kpmg.com/us/en/articles/2026/agentic-ai-untangled.html"
      - title: "Deloitte State of AI in Enterprise 2026"
        url: "https://www.deloitte.com/dk/en/issues/generative-ai/state-of-ai-in-enterprise.html"

  - heading: "Industry Leadership & Use Cases"
    details: |
      ISG State of Agentic AI Market Report (2025): 70% of agentic AI use cases/POCs concentrated in three industries: BFSI (Banking, Financial Services, Insurance), retail, and manufacturing.
      
      Top use cases by industry:
      - Manufacturing: Predictive maintenance (42% of manufacturing use cases), demand forecasting, supply chain optimization
      - Financial Services: Contract intelligence/legal document automation, investment banking presentation generation, fraud detection, trade settlement
      - Customer Service: Multi-language support, customer query resolution (highest ROI velocity—weeks to months)
      - Supply Chain: Autonomous demand planning, inventory optimization, supplier coordination
      - Code Modernization: Legacy code review and translation
      
      Deloitte 2025 survey (1,854 executives): Agentic AI seen as having highest potential impact in:
      - Customer support (primary use case)
      - Supply chain management
      - R&D
      - Knowledge management
      - Cybersecurity
      
      Specific sector adoption patterns:
      - Financial services leading in sophisticated multi-function deployments
      - Retail driving customer-facing agent adoption
      - Manufacturing focusing on predictive/autonomous operations
      - Healthcare emphasizing clinical documentation automation
    sources:
      - title: "ISG State of the Agentic AI Market Report 2025"
        url: "https://isg-one.com/advisory/artificial-intelligence-advisory/state-of-the-agentic-ai-market-report-2025"
      - title: "Deloitte State of AI in Enterprise 2026"
        url: "https://www.deloitte.com/dk/en/issues/generative-ai/state-of-ai-in-enterprise.html"
      - title: "AI Monk: 12 Agentic AI Examples With Measurable ROI (2025-2026)"
        url: "https://aimonk.com/agentic-ai-examples-enterprise-roi-case-studies/"

  - heading: "ROI & Productivity Gains - Quantified Case Studies"
    details: |
      Average ROI metrics across enterprises:
      - Companies report average 171% ROI from agentic AI deployments (Landbase/Arcade data 2025)
      - U.S. enterprises report 192% average ROI, exceeding traditional automation by 3x
      - 74% of executives achieved ROI within first year of deployment (Google Cloud September 2025)
      - 39% reported productivity at least doubled in first year
      
      Named company case studies with verified outcomes:
      
      JPMorgan Chase:
      - Investment Banking Automation: Generates M&A presentations in 30 seconds vs. hours; 450+ active agentic AI deployments in production daily; $18B annual tech budget deployed across agents
      - Contract Intelligence (COiN): Processes 12,000 commercial credit agreements annually, extracts 150 data attributes per document in seconds; 360,000 lawyer-hours reclaimed annually; 80% error reduction; continuous production since 2017
      - Wealth Management AI: Post-meeting note generation and CRM sync; 98% voluntary adoption among financial advisor teams (vs. typical <60% adoption rates)
      
      Klarna (Customer Service):
      - AI agent saves $60 million annually (Q3 2025 earnings disclosure)
      - Equivalent to 853 full-time agent workload replacement
      - Resolution time: 11 minutes → <2 minutes
      - 25% reduction in repeat inquiries
      - Later reintroduced hybrid model (AI for routine queries, humans for complex/emotional issues) after proving fully automated model underperformed on total output
      
      Morgan Stanley:
      - DevGen.AI (Legacy Code Modernization): 280,000 developer hours saved in 2025 alone; reviewed 9+ million lines of code; 15,000 developers deployed to strategic product work instead of manual translation
      - Wealth Management AI Assistant: 98% adoption rate post-meeting note generation across advisor teams
      
      Salesforce (Legal Operations):
      - Internal contract drafting/redlining agent: $5+ million in outside counsel costs eliminated; direct P&L impact
      
      Walmart (Supply Chain):
      - Autonomous demand forecasting agent: 4,700 stores/fulfillment centers connected; autonomous replenishment decisions with zero per-decision human approval loops
      
      General Mills (Supply Chain):
      - $20+ million in supply chain savings since FY2024
      - 5,000+ daily shipments assessed autonomously for routing, timing, vendor performance optimization
      
      Microsoft Copilot Agents:
      - 30-50% reduction in customer service response times (reported by early adopters)
      - Salesforce Agentforce users: ROI in as little as 2 weeks (Valoir Insight Brief, February 2025)
      
      Healthcare Providers:
      - Clinical documentation agents: 42% reduction in documentation time per physician per shift
      - Post-consultation note generation automation
      
      Singapore GovTech (VICA):
      - 800,000+ monthly citizen inquiries resolved without human triage across 60+ government agencies
      - 100+ virtual assistants/chatbots in production (2+ years continuous operation)
      
      Time-to-value patterns:
      - Customer service agents: 2-12 weeks to ROI (fastest)
      - Contract/legal automation: 4-12 weeks
      - Supply chain orchestration: 6-18 months (requires data infrastructure maturity)
      - Enterprise onboarding typical: 32-64 weeks from discovery to full deployment
      
      Failure risk: 60% of DIY AI initiatives fail to scale past pilot stage due to unclear ROI definition
    sources:
      - title: "AI Monk: 12 Agentic AI Examples With Measurable ROI (2025-2026)"
        url: "https://aimonk.com/agentic-ai-examples-enterprise-roi-case-studies/"
      - title: "Google Cloud ROI of AI Study"
        url: "https://cloud.google.com/transform/roi-of-ai-how-agents-help-business"
      - title: "Landbase Agentic AI Statistics"
        url: "https://www.landbase.com/blog/agentic-ai-statistics"
      - title: "Klarna Q3 2025 Earnings - AI Agent Savings"
        url: "https://finance.yahoo.com/news/klarna-says-ai-agent-doing-070000869.html"
      - title: "Morgan Stanley DevGen.AI - 280,000 Developer Hours Saved"
        url: "https://www.businessinsider.com/devgen-ai-tool-saved-morgan-stanley-280-000-hours-jobs-2025-7"
      - title: "Salesforce Valoir Insight: Accelerating Agentic AI Time to Value"
        url: "https://www.salesforce.com/en-us/wp-content/uploads/sites/4/assets/pdf/agentforce/Valoir-Insight-Brief-10_25-Update-Accelerating-Agentic-Time-to-Value.pdf"

  - heading: "Main Barriers to Adoption"
    details: |
      Security and governance concerns dominate barrier lists across all surveys:
      
      McKinsey (October 2025 "Deploying Agentic AI with Safety and Security" playbook):
      - Nearly two-thirds cite security and risk as top barriers to scaling
      - 79% of organizations deploying agentic AI have NO security policies in place (Pixee AI, January 2026)
      - 86% of agents deployed without security approval (Gravitee/CSA 2026)
      - Governance/trust framework gaps cited as critical blockers
      
      Gartner (June 2025 prediction):
      - Over 40% of agentic AI projects will be canceled by end of 2027
      - Primary reasons: escalating costs, unclear business value, inadequate risk controls
      - Integration complexity and multi-system coordination challenges
      
      Deloitte 2025 Enterprise Survey (1,854 execs):
      - Insufficient worker skills largest barrier (51% cite)
      - Unclear use cases or business value (common among 39% in experimental phases)
      - Integration with legacy systems (cited as major friction)
      - Governance, risk, and compliance framework gaps
      - AI reliability concerns (hallucinations, inconsistency)
      
      Forbes Analysis (September 2025):
      - Hallucination risks (data accuracy/trust)
      - Data privacy and copyright concerns
      - Integration complexity
      - Talent scarcity (AI specialists expensive/rare)
      - Regulatory uncertainty
      
      MIT Sloan/BCG Report (November 2025):
      - Organizations adopting agents "well before they have a strategy in place"
      - Autonomous decision-making requires new governance models
      - Monitoring, auditability, and human oversight mechanisms lacking
      - Talent shortage in agent design/operations
      
      Capgemini Rise of Agentic AI (July 2025):
      - Trust in AI agents has fallen sharply (14% of executives trust agents fully)
      - Ethical AI and autonomous system risks not yet addressed
      - Reliability concerns (agents making uncontrolled decisions)
      
      Common integration barriers:
      - 60% of custom AI development time consumed by system integration/API connectivity
      - Legacy data/infrastructure architectures cannot support real-time autonomous AI
      - Multi-agent orchestration complexity
      - Data quality and readiness (organizations rushing deployment without data prep)
    sources:
      - title: "McKinsey: Deploying Agentic AI with Safety and Security (October 2025)"
        url: "https://www.mckinsey.com/capabilities/risk-and-resilience/our-insights/deploying-agentic-ai-with-safety-and-security-a-playbook-for-technology-leaders"
      - title: "Gartner: 40% of Agentic AI Projects Will Be Canceled by 2027"
        url: "https://www.gartner.com/en/newsroom/press-releases/2025-06-25-gartner-predicts-over-40-percent-of-agentic-ai-projects-will-be-canceled-by-end-of-2027"
      - title: "Deloitte State of AI in Enterprise 2026"
        url: "https://www.deloitte.com/dk/en/issues/generative-ai/state-of-ai-in-enterprise.html"
      - title: "Pixee AI: Agentic AI Governance Gap - 79% Lack Policy"
        url: "https://www.pixee.ai/blog/agentic-ai-governance-gap-strategic-framework-2026"
      - title: "MIT Sloan/BCG: Emerging Agentic Enterprise (November 2025)"
        url: "https://sloanreview.mit.edu/projects/the-emerging-agentic-enterprise-how-leaders-must-navigate-a-new-age-of-ai/"
      - title: "Capgemini: Rise of Agentic AI (July 2025)"
        url: "https://www.capgemini.com/insights/research-library/ai-agents/"

  - heading: "Build vs. Buy vs. Borrow Strategy Shift"
    details: |
      Dramatic reversal in enterprise strategy within 12 months (2024-2025):
      
      2024 baseline:
      - 47% built internally
      - 53% purchased
      
      2025 current state:
      - 24% built internally (down 49%)
      - 76% purchased (up 43%)
      - Total enterprise generative AI spending: $37 billion (3.2x growth YoY)
      
      KPMG Q3 2025 AI Pulse Survey:
      - 57% of organizations now favor blended build-and-buy approach (hybrid strategy)
      - Up from 51% at start of 2025
      - Organizations increasingly combining platform purchases with custom integrations
      
      Reasons for buy-first shift:
      - Development costs $100K-$500K+ for enterprise-grade solutions
      - Ongoing maintenance $5K-$20K monthly
      - Compliance/security costs $10K-$100K annually (regulated industries)
      - Technical debt: 65% of total costs materialize after deployment
      - Time-to-value: Off-the-shelf solutions deliver weeks vs. months for custom builds
      - Integration complexity: 60% of dev time spent on system connectivity (not AI logic)
      - Talent scarcity: Specialized AI expertise too expensive to scale internally
      - 60% of DIY initiatives fail to scale past pilot stage
      
      Platform advantages cited:
      - Pre-built integrations (1500+ connectors)
      - Automatic updates and continuous improvement
      - Battle-tested reliability at scale
      - Built-in governance and security frameworks
      - Multi-agent orchestration capabilities
      
      When custom build still makes sense (24% exception):
      - Proprietary algorithms defining competitive advantage
      - Extreme compliance requirements (highly regulated)
      - Deep legacy system dependencies
      - Unlimited budgets (R&D-focused organizations)
      
      Hybrid approach best practice:
      - Leverage platforms for core functionality
      - Add custom integration layers via APIs
      - White-label deployment where needed
      - Open-source compatibility for extensibility
    sources:
      - title: "Beam AI: Great AI Flip - 76% of Enterprises Now Buy vs Build"
        url: "https://beam.ai/agentic-insights/the-great-ai-flip-why-76-of-enterprises-stopped-building-ai-in-house"
      - title: "KPMG: Agentic AI Untangled - Build/Buy/Borrow Q3 2025"
        url: "https://kpmg.com/us/en/articles/2026/agentic-ai-untangled.html"
      - title: "Aisera: Build vs Buy AI Agents Guide 2026"
        url: "https://aisera.com/blog/build-vs-buy-ai/"

  - heading: "Deployment Timeline: Pilot to Production"
    details: |
      Time-to-value varies dramatically by use case maturity and data readiness:
      
      Fastest deployments (high-ROI/high-volume processes):
      - Customer service automation: 2-4 weeks to measurable ROI
      - Contract review/legal document processing: 4-12 weeks
      - Chatbot/conversational AI: 2-8 weeks
      - Reason: Pre-existing KPIs (handle time, CSAT, repeat contact rate) visible within weeks
      
      Medium-velocity deployments:
      - Code generation/developer tools: 4-12 weeks
      - Financial automation (fraud detection, trade settlement): 6-16 weeks
      - Reason: Clear baseline metrics, bounded workflows
      
      Longest deployments (complex orchestration):
      - Supply chain optimization: 12-18+ months (often 6-12 month data prep)
      - Multi-system ERP integration: 12-24 months
      - Clinical/healthcare deployment: 12-18 months (regulatory, safety validation)
      - Reason: Data infrastructure maturity required; extended validation cycles; autonomous decisions at scale need governance buildup
      
      Enterprise onboarding typical timeline:
      - Discovery to full production: 32-64 weeks average
      - Pilot phase: 4-16 weeks
      - Full deployment: 8-16 weeks post-pilot approval
      - Governance setup: 2-8 weeks before production cutover
      
      Deployment success factors:
      - Pre-defined KPIs/ROI metrics (before code runs) = faster and higher success
      - Data infrastructure readiness (60% of DIY failures stem from data pipeline issues)
      - Clear ownership and accountability model
      - Governance framework established before rollout (not retrofitted)
      - SaaStr playbook (2025): "Zero to 20 AI agents in 10 months" achievable with platform approach

  - heading: "Monetization Models & Pricing Strategies"
    details: |
      Enterprise agentic AI pricing has not yet converged on a single model; vendors experimenting with multiple approaches:
      
      Salesforce Agentforce (market leader, multiple iterations in 2025):
      - Initial model (Q1 2025): $2 per conversation
      - Evolved to Flex Credits (May 2025): $0.10 per action consumed (consumption-based)
      - Current model (Summer 2025 onwards): 
        * Per-user license: $125/user/month for Sales Cloud/Service Cloud add-on
        * Flex Credits: Variable spend model allowing cross-team/process deployment
        * Base pricing increase: 6% average across product line (August 1, 2025)
      - AI and Data Cloud segment ARR contribution: $900M annual recurring revenue (FY2025 Q4)
      
      Pricing model distribution observed across vendors (2025):
      - 45% of vendors use usage-based/consumption pricing (most vulnerable to commoditization)
      - 20% use per-agent licensing (FTE replacement positioning)
      - 15% use outcome-based pricing (tied to measurable business results)
      - 20% hybrid models (combination of seat licenses + consumption overages)
      
      Per-agent pricing models:
      - Treats AI agent as "digital employee" with monthly per-seat fee
      - Ranges: $100-300/agent/month observed in market
      - Aligns with FTE replacement narrative but caps addressable market
      
      Consumption-based models:
      - Per-action, per-task, or per-token pricing
      - Cost scales with usage (favorable for low-volume pilots, risky at scale)
      - Examples: Anthropic Claude API ($0.80-$3/million tokens depending on model/direction)
      
      Outcome-based pricing (emerging):
      - Charges tied to measurable ROI (hours saved, cost reduction, revenue increase)
      - Rare; requires deep integration and transparent metrics
      - More common in high-value enterprise contracts
      
      Service/consulting layer:
      - Agencies and SI partners charging $100K-$500K+ for enterprise deployment
      - $5K-$20K monthly ongoing management/optimization fees
      - Compliance and governance setup: $10K-$100K per engagement
      
      Market observations:
      - Vendors historically used $2/conversation pricing (too low, unprofitable at scale)
      - Shift to consumption-based units ($0.10 per action) more sustainable but unpredictable for enterprises
      - Per-seat licensing ($125/user/month) offers revenue predictability but limits adoption
      - Hybrid models gaining traction as enterprise preference (capped base cost + overages)
      - No dominant standard yet; vendor-specific pricing as competitive differentiator
```
